---
title: "The Slow Return: Tracking the Recovery of Auckland's Foot Traffic"
subtitle: "Analysing Auckland CBD's pedestrian data from 2020-2024"
author: "Georgia Short"
format:
  pdf:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    geometry: margin=2.5cm
---

```{python}
#| eval: True

#=========================
#     IMPORT PACKAGES
#=========================
import pandas as pd
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mtick
import requests
import time
import zipfile
import io
import geopandas as gpd
import folium
import branca.colormap as cm

#=========================
#     BUILD FUNCTIONS
#=========================
#First function
def count_missing(df):
    """
    Count missing values in each column of a DataFrame.

    Parameters
    ----------
    df : pandas.DataFrame
        The input dataset.

    Returns
    -------
    pandas.Series
        Number of missing values per column.
    """
    return df.isnull().sum()

#Second function
def footfall_level(count):
    """
    Categorise footfall into low, medium, or high levels.

    Parameters
    ----------
    count : int or float
        Footfall count for a given observation.

    Returns
    -------
    str
        Category label: 'Low', 'Medium', or 'High'.
    """
    if count < 300:
        return "Low"
    elif count < 1000:
        return "Medium"
    else:
        return "High"

#Third function
def compute_recovery(df, base_year="2020", compare_year="2024"):
    """
    Compute percentage recovery between two years for each sensor or SA2.

    Parameters
    ----------
    df : pandas.DataFrame
        Wide-format DataFrame with year columns (e.g., '2020', '2024').
    base_year : str
        The reference year for comparison.
    compare_year : str
        The year to compare against the base year.

    Returns
    -------
    pandas.DataFrame
        Original DataFrame with an added 'pct_recovery' column.
    """
    df = df.copy()
    df["pct_recovery"] = (
        (df[compare_year] - df[base_year]) / df[base_year] * 100
    )
    return df

#=========================
#       DATA IMPORT 
#=========================
files = [                   #Assign all csv's to analyise to one name
    'akl_ped-2020.csv',
    'akl_ped-2021.csv',
    'akl_ped-2022.csv',
    'akl_ped-2023.csv',
    'akl_ped-2024.csv'
]
df = [pd.read_csv(f) for f in files] #Read csv files into dataframe
ped = pd.concat(df, ignore_index=True) #Combine into one datafrane

sensor_locations = pd.read_csv("akl_ped-Geodata.csv")

```


```{python}
#| eval: False

#Explore the structure
print(ped.shape) #Look at the shape of the data
count_missing(ped)  #Identify missing values using function 1
ped.info()
ped.head() #Check data has loaded correctly
ped.tail()
ped.describe()

```
```{python}
#| eval: True

#=========================
#      DATA CLEANING 
#=========================
# Clean non-data rows first, then convert Date to datetime
ped = ped.dropna(subset=['Date', 'Time']) 
ped = ped[ped['Date'] != 'Daylight Savings']
# Ensure Date is datetime
ped['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(ped['Date'], format = 'mixed')
# Extract useful temporal features
ped["month"] = ped["Date"].dt.month #Month
ped["day_of_week"] = pd.to_datetime(ped["Date"]).dt.day_name() #Day of week
ped["hour"] = ped["Time"].str.split(":").str[0].astype(int) #Hour

ped = ped.rename(columns={"59 High Stret": "59 High Street"}) #Correct errors in names
ped = ped.drop(columns=[ #Removed theses sensors data due to being unavailable during some of the time period
    "188 Quay Street Lower Albert (EW)", 
    "188 Quay Street Lower Albert (NS)",
    "107 Quay Street"]
    ) 
#Fix object to interger
# Convert all sensor columns to numeric (fixes 150 K Road)
sensor_cols = [
    c for c in ped.columns 
    if c not in ["Date", "Time", "month", "day_of_week", "hour"]
]

ped[sensor_cols] = ped[sensor_cols].apply(
    pd.to_numeric, errors="coerce"
)
ped.dtypes #check if fix worked
count_missing(ped) # Check for missing values across the entire DataFrame

ped_interp = ped.copy()
ped_interp[sensor_cols] = ped_interp[sensor_cols].interpolate(method="linear") #Fill missing values with a mean using interpolate
ped_interp.describe()
count_missing(ped_interp)
ped_long = ped_interp.melt(    #Convert wide to long format
    id_vars=["Date", "Time", "month", "day_of_week", "hour"],
    value_vars=sensor_cols,
    var_name='Location',
    value_name='Count'
)
print(ped_long.head(10))
print(f"\nWide shape: {ped.shape}")
print(f"Long shape: {ped_long.shape}")
count_missing(ped_long)

ped_long["year"] = ped_long["Date"].dt.year
yearly = (
    ped_long
    .groupby("year")["Count"]
    .sum()
    .reset_index()
)
ped_long["year"] = ped_long["Date"].dt.year
print(yearly)
#Fix wrong year
mask_2017 = ped_long["Date"].dt.year == 2017
ped_long.loc[mask_2017, "Date"] = ped_long.loc[mask_2017, "Date"].apply(
    lambda d: d.replace(year=2020)
)
ped_long["year"] = ped_long["Date"].dt.year
yearly = (
    ped_long
    .groupby("year")["Count"]
    .sum()
    .reset_index()
)

print(yearly)

```


```{python}
#| eval: True

#=========================
#     API INTEGRATION 
#=========================

from config import API_KEY 

LAYER_ID = 111227   # SA2 2023 layer
BASE_URL = "https://datafinder.stats.govt.nz/services/api/v1.x"

HEADERS = {
    "Authorization": f"key {API_KEY}",
    "Content-Type": "application/json",
}

payload = {
    "crs": "EPSG:2193",   # Stats NZ default (NZTM)
    "formats": {"vector": "application/x-ogc-gpkg"},
    "items": [{"item": f"{BASE_URL}/layers/{LAYER_ID}/"}],
}

response = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/exports/", json=payload, headers=HEADERS)
response.raise_for_status()

export_data = response.json()
export_url = export_data["url"]

print("Export job created:", export_url)
print("Initial state:", export_data["state"])

while True:
    status = requests.get(export_url, headers=HEADERS).json()
    state = status["state"]
    print("Export state:", state)

    if state == "complete":
        download_url = status["download_url"]
        break
    elif state == "failed":
        raise RuntimeError("Export failed.")

    time.sleep(2)

zip_path = "sa2_2023.zip"
dl = requests.get(download_url, headers=HEADERS, stream=True)
dl.raise_for_status()

with open(zip_path, "wb") as f:
    for chunk in dl.iter_content(chunk_size=8192):
        f.write(chunk)

print("ZIP downloaded:", zip_path)

extract_folder = "sa2_2023/"
with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_path) as zf:
    gpkg_name = [n for n in zf.namelist() if n.endswith(".gpkg")][0]
    zf.extractall(extract_folder)

print("Extracted:", gpkg_name)

sa2_2023 = gpd.read_file(f"{extract_folder}{gpkg_name}")
print("Original CRS:", sa2_2023.crs)
print(sa2_2023.shape)

sa2_2023_4326 = sa2_2023.to_crs("EPSG:4326")
print("Reprojected CRS:", sa2_2023_4326.crs)

output_path = "sa2_2023_4326.gpkg"
sa2_2023_4326.to_file(output_path, driver="GPKG")

print("Saved:", output_path)
```

```{python} 
#| echo: false

# Compute monthly totals by year
monthly_year = (
    ped_long
    .groupby(["year", "month"])["Count"]
    .sum()
    .reset_index()
)

plt.figure(figsize=(12,6))

sns.lineplot(
    data=monthly_year,
    x="month",
    y="Count",
    hue="year",
    palette="colorblind",
    marker="o"
)

plt.title("Monthly Footfall Trends in Auckland CBD (2020–2024)")
plt.xlabel("Month")
plt.ylabel("Total Footfall")

plt.xticks(
    ticks=range(1,13),
    labels=["Jan","Feb","Mar","Apr","May","Jun","Jul","Aug","Sep","Oct","Nov","Dec"]
)

# Force linear scale (just in case)
plt.yscale("linear")

# Format y-axis in millions
plt.gca().yaxis.set_major_formatter(mtick.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f'{x/1e6:.1f}M'))

plt.grid(True)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

ped_long["footfall_level"] = ped_long["Count"].apply(footfall_level)

ped_2020 = ped_long[ped_long["year"] == 2020]
ped_2024 = ped_long[ped_long["year"] == 2024]

dist_2020 = ped_2020["footfall_level"].value_counts(normalize=True) * 100
dist_2024 = ped_2024["footfall_level"].value_counts(normalize=True) * 100

print("2020 distribution (%):")
print(dist_2020)

print("\n2024 distribution (%):")
print(dist_2024)

compare_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "2020": dist_2020,
    "2024": dist_2024
})

compare_df.plot(kind="bar", figsize=(8,5), color=["#9999cc", "#ff9966"])
plt.title("Distribution of Footfall Levels: 2020 vs 2024")
plt.ylabel("Percentage of Observations (%)")
plt.xticks(rotation=0)
plt.grid(axis="y")
plt.show()

# Clean column names
sensor_locations = sensor_locations.rename(columns={
    "Address": "Location",
    "Latitude": "lat",
    "Longitude": "lon"
})

# Fix the typo so it matches your cleaned ped_long names
sensor_locations["Location"] = sensor_locations["Location"].replace({
    "59 High Stret": "59 High Street"
})

sensor_locations.head()

# Drop rows with missing lat/lon 
sensor_clean = sensor_locations.dropna(subset=["lat", "lon"]).copy()

# Convert to GeoDataFrame 
sensors_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    sensor_clean,
    geometry=gpd.points_from_xy(sensor_clean["lon"], sensor_clean["lat"]),
    crs="EPSG:4326"
)

sensors_gdf.head()
sensor_locations.describe()

#Convert to wide format
sensor_year_wide = (
    ped_long
    .groupby(["Location", "year"])["Count"]
    .sum()
    .reset_index()
    .pivot(index="Location", columns="year", values="Count")
    .reset_index()
)

# Convert year columns to STRINGS (critical fix)
sensor_year_wide = sensor_year_wide.rename(columns={
    2020: "2020",
    2021: "2021",
    2022: "2022",
    2023: "2023",
    2024: "2024"
})

sensor_year_wide = compute_recovery(sensor_year_wide)
# Merge into sensor GeoDataFrame
sensors_recovery = sensors_gdf.merge(
    sensor_year_wide[["Location", "2020", "2024", "pct_recovery"]],
    on="Location",
    how="left"
).dropna(subset=["pct_recovery"])

# Spatial join: assign each sensor to an SA2
joined_sa2 = gpd.sjoin(
    sensors_gdf,
    sa2_2023_4326,
    how="left",
    predicate="within"
)

# Merge ped_long with SA2 info
ped_sa2 = ped_long.merge(
    joined_sa2[["Location", "SA22023_V1_00_NAME"]],
    on="Location",
    how="left"
)

# Total footfall per SA2 per year
sa2_year = (
    ped_sa2
    .groupby(["SA22023_V1_00_NAME", "year"])["Count"]
    .sum()
    .reset_index()
)

# Pivot to wide format
sa2_year_wide = sa2_year.pivot(
    index="SA22023_V1_00_NAME",
    columns="year",
    values="Count"
).reset_index()

# Convert year columns to STRINGS (critical fix)
sa2_year_wide = sa2_year_wide.rename(columns={
    2020: "2020",
    2021: "2021",
    2022: "2022",
    2023: "2023",
    2024: "2024"
})

# Compute SA2 recovery
sa2_year_wide = compute_recovery(sa2_year_wide)

# Merge ALL year totals + recovery into SA2 polygons
sa2_recovery = sa2_2023_4326.merge(
    sa2_year_wide[
        ["SA22023_V1_00_NAME", "2020", "2021", "2022", "2023", "2024", "pct_recovery"]
    ],
    on="SA22023_V1_00_NAME",
    how="left"
)

# Remove SA2s with no recovery value
sa2_recovery_clean = sa2_recovery.dropna(subset=["pct_recovery"]).copy()

m = folium.Map(
    location=[sensors_gdf["lat"].mean(), sensors_gdf["lon"].mean()],
    zoom_start=15,
    tiles="CartoDB Positron"
)

colormap = cm.linear.PuOr_11.scale(
    sa2_recovery_clean["pct_recovery"].min(),
    sa2_recovery_clean["pct_recovery"].max()
)

colormap.caption = "SA2 Footfall Recovery (2024 vs 2020)"
colormap.add_to(m)

tooltip = folium.GeoJsonTooltip(
    fields=["SA22023_V1_00_NAME", "2020", "2021", "2022", "2023", "2024", "pct_recovery"],
    aliases=["SA2:", "2020:", "2021:", "2022:", "2023:", "2024:", "Recovery (%):"],
    localize=True
)

style_fn = lambda feature: {
    "fillColor": colormap(float(feature["properties"]["pct_recovery"])),
    "color": "#222222",      # darker outline for visibility
    "weight": 0.4,           # thinner borders
    "fillOpacity": 0.85,     # stronger fill
}

highlight_fn = lambda feature: {
    "fillColor": colormap(float(feature["properties"]["pct_recovery"])),
    "color": "black",
    "weight": 2,
    "fillOpacity": 0.95,
}

folium.GeoJson(
    sa2_recovery_clean,
    style_function=style_fn,
    highlight_function=highlight_fn,
    tooltip=tooltip
).add_to(m)

for _, row in sensors_recovery.iterrows():
    folium.CircleMarker(
        location=[row.lat, row.lon],
        radius=6,
        fill=True,
        fill_color="black",
        color="white",
        weight=1,
        tooltip=(
            f"<b>{row['Location']}</b><br>"
            f"2020 total: {row['2020']:,}<br>"
            f"2024 total: {row['2024']:,}<br>"
            f"Recovery: {row['pct_recovery']:.1f}%"
        )
    ).add_to(m)

```

``` {python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

m._repr_html_()
```
